hero.title: Forecast for [[landing-sol-usdt|SOL/USDT]] and validation on history
subtitleParagraphs: SolSignal 1D is a system that evaluates, on each [[landing-time-horizon|trading day]], where [[landing-sol-usdt|SOL/USDT]] is more likely to move during that day ([[landing-day-up|Up]] / [[landing-day-down|Down]] / [[landing-day-flat|Sideways]]) and converts that forecast into a clear trading scenario: entry, position size, and risk limits.

The website shows not only the current forecast but also how that same strategy behaved on history starting from April 13, 2022: where it earned, where it was wrong, what [[drawdown|drawdown]] it went through, and how quickly it recovered after weak periods.
bullets: Decision for the nearest trading day: position direction, [[landing-model-confidence|confidence]], and risk limits.
An archive of past forecasts where each date can be compared with the realized outcome of the same window.
Historical validation with return, [[drawdown|drawdown]], [[liquidation|liquidations]], and [[recovery|recovery speed after major drawdowns]].

audience.title: Who this format is built for
audience.subtitle: The product can be read on three levels: business outcome, analytical validation, and technical detail for risk and models.

business.title: Business
body: The business layer answers whether the system produces a repeatable result and what level of risk stands behind that return.
The key reading layer includes not only headline percentages, but also [[drawdown|drawdown]], [[liquidation|liquidations]], and stability across long history.

analyst.title: Analyst
body: The analytical layer preserves forecasts by date, a historical summary, and reason slices so the result can be validated rather than taken on trust.
That makes it possible to see where the effect is produced: in the forecast, in the risk filter, in the [[landing-trading-policy|trading policy]], or in a specific market regime.

quantRisk.title: Quant and risk
body: The technical layer provides source tables and explain material so headline numbers can be decomposed into concrete metrics.
For deeper validation the website exposes [[landing-policy-branch-mega|Policy Branch Mega]], [[landing-guardrail|guardrail]], [[landing-hotspots|hotspots]], and related diagnostic pages.

workflow.title: How one forecast moves through the full cycle
workflow.subtitle: The project follows the same cycle for every trading day. Model details, thresholds, and risk rules are explained through tooltips and explain pages.

forecast.title: Forecast for the trading day
body: At the start of the trading window the system calculates a [[landing-forecast|forecast]] for [[landing-sol-usdt|SOL/USDT]].
The output contains position direction, [[landing-model-confidence|confidence]], and risk constraints for that day.

archive.title: Archived before outcome review
body: After publication the calculation enters the [[landing-prediction-history|forecast archive]].
Each date stores both the forecast itself and the realized outcome of that same window after it closes.

validate.title: Validated on long history
body: The same strategy also goes through [[landing-backtest-summary|historical validation]] across a long series of trading days.
This layer shows not only return, but also the cost of that result through risk and weak periods.

proof.title: Which evidence supports the result
proof.subtitle: Trust here rests on three sources: a preserved archive, long-history validation, and explicit [[landing-truthfulness|data truthfulness]] rules.

archive.title: Forecast archive
archive.description: The archive confirms that past forecasts are preserved by date and can be compared with the realized outcome of the same window.
archive.errorDescription: The archive index could not be loaded for the proof card.

backtest.title: Validation on history
backtest.description: The summary shows how this strategy behaves on long history: across return, risk, and emergency events.
backtest.errorDescription: Headline metrics could not be extracted from the summary report.

truth.title: Data truthfulness
truth.description: The truthfulness section describes which data is available at calculation time and how the project separates the forecast from future outcome.

tooltipRules.solana: Solana is the network and SOL is its native asset. In this product the important object is not the network itself but the [[landing-sol-usdt|SOL/USDT]] pair used for forecast and historical validation.
tooltipRules.solUsdt:
[[landing-sol-usdt|SOL/USDT]] is a trading pair on Binance. It shows the price of the SOL coin in USDT, meaning how many units of USDT the Binance market gives for 1 SOL.

USDT is a crypto token whose price usually stays close to 1 US dollar.

The public website currently concentrates on this single pair so [[landing-current-prediction|current prediction]], [[landing-prediction-history|prediction history]], and [[backtest|backtest]] stay comparable on one instrument without mixing multiple coins.
tooltipRules.mlProject:
An ML project is a product where the key decision is built by a machine-learning model rather than by a manual analyst rule.

In this project the model takes historical market data, turns it into [[landing-features|features]], and produces a [[landing-forecast|forecast]] for one trading day.

Alongside the current result, the website also shows the historical validation of that strategy.
tooltipRules.mlModel: An algorithm trained on historical data that estimates the scenario for one trading day on [[landing-sol-usdt|SOL/USDT]]. Training and [[backtest|backtest]] use the same time window that later appears on the website as the forecast day.
tooltipRules.btc: Bitcoin. In this product it works as an external reference for the [[landing-ml-model|ML model]] alongside [[landing-solana|Solana]], making it easier to see whether [[landing-sol-usdt|SOL/USDT]] moves with the broader crypto market.
tooltipRules.macroIndicators: External market and economic signals that help the [[landing-ml-model|model]] distinguish local [[landing-solana|Solana]] movement from a broader market regime.
tooltipRules.features: Features are numeric inputs for the [[landing-ml-model|ML model]]. They are built from price, volatility, [[landing-btc|BTC]] behavior, and other observable factors, then transformed into a [[landing-forecast|forecast]].
tooltipRules.signal: The output of the [[landing-ml-model|model]] for one [[landing-time-horizon|NY session]]: position direction, [[landing-model-confidence|confidence]], and risk constraints. This output is then passed through the [[landing-trading-policy|trading policy]].
tooltipRules.forecast: The expected scenario for [[landing-sol-usdt|SOL/USDT]] over one working trading window. On the website this format is used both for the nearest live calculation and for past dates inside [[landing-prediction-history|prediction history]].
tooltipRules.currentPrediction: A page with the nearest forecast for [[landing-sol-usdt|SOL/USDT]] for one New York trading day. It shows the main calculation for the current trading window and, when published in advance, the [[landing-early-preview|early preview]] for the same day.
tooltipRules.predictionHistory: An archive of past forecasts rebuilt from the [[backtest|backtest]] by date. Each date contains both the forecast itself and the realized result of the corresponding trading window. The history available on the website starts on April 13, 2022.
tooltipRules.backtestSummary: A historical validation page for this strategy. It is read not only through return, but also through [[drawdown|drawdown]], [[liquidation|liquidations]], emergency risk, and [[recovery|recovery speed after major drawdowns]].
tooltipRules.diagnostics: A set of pages showing where the result is lost: at entry, in the risk filter, in [[landing-no-trade|no-trade]] days, or in specific market regimes.
tooltipRules.analysis: A comparison section for strategies, day slices, and confidence slices. It is used when one headline number is no longer enough.
tooltipRules.explain: A plain-language section for project terms and mechanics: models, forecast windows, tables, and report-reading logic.
tooltipRules.truthfulness: Documentation about data truthfulness: which data was available at forecast time, how the day outcome is calculated separately, and why future information must not flow back into the decision.
tooltipRules.timeHorizon:
The working window is one trading day of the US session: from the beginning of the working day in New York to the end of that same day.

The current forecast, historical labels, and [[backtest|backtest]] all use this same window, so every section compares the same type of day rather than different parts of the 24-hour cycle.

[[landing-time-horizon-why|Why?]]

The [[landing-early-preview|early preview]] refers to the same day, but is calculated earlier, before the main session starts.
tooltipRules.timeHorizonWhy:
Because the start of the US session usually brings more liquidity and stronger intraday movement than the quieter hours before it.

That makes volatility closer to the conditions where the strategy actually has to make a decision, and metrics such as the [[atr-indicator|ATR indicator]] describe the active market better than a calm slice of the day.

For that reason the models are trained and validated on the part of the day where market movement is more likely to move from noise into a tradable scenario.
tooltipRules.dayUp: Up is the day scenario where the model expects price to move mainly upward inside the working trading window.
tooltipRules.dayDown: Down is the day scenario where the model expects price to move mainly downward inside the working trading window.
tooltipRules.dayFlat: Sideways is the day scenario without a strong directional move, where price is more likely to stay inside a range instead of breaking strongly up or down.
tooltipRules.pathBasedLabeling: A way to label historical days not only by close price but also by the path price traveled inside the window. This helps the [[landing-ml-model|model]] separate a tradable move from a formally similar but unstable scenario.
tooltipRules.earlyPreview: The early preview is a forecast for the same trading window, but published in advance before the main NY window opens. It is marked separately because it is needed earlier than the main calculation.
tooltipRules.microModel:
The micro model is applied only to the subset of days where the base daily model returned FLAT.

Its job is to refine a weak directional bias inside a sideways scenario, not to reclassify every day from scratch.

On the page this is visible in the Day -> Day+Micro transition.
tooltipRules.multiLayer: A day / micro / SL pipeline. The daily model sets the class relative to [[min-move|MinMove]], the [[landing-micro-model|micro model]] refines only the FLAT subset, and the SL layer adds a risk overlay to the final interpretation.
tooltipRules.slRisk: Metrics that describe fast stop-loss risk and days where entries are especially vulnerable. They help show when the strategy is more likely to fall into protective constraints.
tooltipRules.guardrail: A protective filter that tries to keep the strategy out of bad days. It is read through [[landing-specificity|specificity]], block rate, and overall economic impact.
tooltipRules.specificity: A filter-purity metric for bad days: it shows how cleanly [[landing-guardrail|guardrail]] separates truly problematic regimes from normal ones.
tooltipRules.blameSplit: A split of failure source by decision layer: signal error, filter error, or trading-rule error. It helps separate a weak [[landing-ml-model|model]] from an overly strict [[landing-trading-policy|trading policy]].
tooltipRules.hotspots: Slices where the strategy repeatedly underperforms: too few trades, too much [[landing-no-trade|NoTrade]], or frequent decisions against the market. Useful for finding recurring weak regimes.
tooltipRules.pfi: Permutation Feature Importance — a report showing how much model quality degrades when one feature is shuffled. The larger the drop, the more important the feature is for the [[landing-forecast|forecast]].
tooltipRules.confusionMatrix: A table of correct and incorrect classifications. In this product it helps show where the [[landing-ml-model|model]] and [[landing-guardrail|guardrail]] correctly identify good and bad days.
tooltipRules.modelMetrics: Model-quality metrics: scenario accuracy, stability across day types, and behavior under risk. They are usually read together with [[landing-confusion-matrix|confusion matrix]] and [[landing-pfi|PFI]].
tooltipRules.policyBranchMega: The full historical table for [[policy|Policy]] / [[branch|Branch]] combinations. It brings together return, drawdown, liquidations, LONG/SHORT structure, and other metrics for each configuration.
tooltipRules.noTrade: A day with no trade. It happens when the strategy has no direction, a risk filter blocks the entry, or the final [[cap-fraction|capital share]] becomes zero.
tooltipRules.attribution: A breakdown of which causes shaped the final decision. It helps show whether [[landing-signal|signal]], risk filtering, or the [[landing-trading-policy|trading policy]] had the strongest effect.
tooltipRules.featureImportance: An estimate of how strongly each feature affects the [[landing-ml-model|model]] decision. On the website this view is represented through [[landing-pfi|PFI]] and explain reports.
tooltipRules.predictionAggregation: The layer that combines several model signals into one final day-level decision. It is used to compare individual models with the final [[landing-forecast|forecast]].
tooltipRules.allHistory: A view over the full available history without selecting separate periods. It is used to compare [[landing-trading-policy|trading policies]] on the same time scale.
tooltipRules.baselineBacktest: A control [[landing-backtest|backtest]] with the base rule configuration. It serves as the reference point for comparing other modes.
tooltipRules.experimentalBacktest: An exploratory [[landing-backtest|backtest]] for new or unstable configurations. It should be read as a research scenario, not as the primary business benchmark.
tooltipRules.modelConfidence: A numeric estimate of how confident the model is in the selected day scenario. In this product it affects risk sizing and, in some modes, [[cap-fraction|capital share]] and dynamic exit control.
tooltipRules.liquidationBuffer: The distance between the current price and the level where the position would be forcibly closed. In [[anti-direction|anti-direction]], this buffer acts as a safety filter: if it is too small relative to [[min-move|MinMove]], inversion becomes risky.

tour.title: How the website is grouped by task
tour.subtitle: Navigation is organized around three scenarios: the nearest day, historical validation, and reason analysis.

today.title: Today
today.description: The nearest trading day and the final decision for the current window.

forecast.title: Forecast and decision
forecast.description: The nearest market calculation: forecast, risk limits, and the final decision for the current day.

validation.title: Validation
validation.description: The archive of past forecasts and the long-history validation layer.

history.title: Forecast archive
history.description: Past forecasts by date with the realized outcome of that same window.

summary.title: Backtest summary
summary.description: Long-history validation through return, risk, and stability.

mega.title: Policy Branch Mega
mega.description: The full configuration table where return is read together with emergency risk.

causes.title: Reasons
causes.description: Reasons for strong and weak results, no-trade periods, and risk filters.

diagnostics.title: Diagnostics
diagnostics.description: A general view of weak points across models, filters, and market regimes.

guardrail.title: Guardrail and risk
guardrail.description: The protective filter and the metric cost of its blocks.

hotspots.title: No-trade and hotspots
hotspots.description: Recurring weak regimes, no-trade days, and low-quality market zones.

demo.title: One configuration through the risk lens
demo.subtitle: One row from [[landing-policy-branch-mega|Policy Branch Mega]] shows how the project stores outcome and risk for a single configuration across the full history.
demo.note: The [[total-pnl|TotalPnl%]] figure is not self-sufficient here: it has to be read together with [[drawdown|drawdown]], [[liquidation|liquidations]], recovery speed, and trade activity.

errors.loadDescription: Policy Branch Mega data could not be loaded for the configuration card.
errors.structureTitle: Mini demo structure could not be resolved
errors.structureDescription: The received report could not be transformed into the compact configuration card.
errors.metaDescription: The required period fields could not be extracted from the selected row.
errors.metricsDescription: The metric set required for the compact card could not be extracted.

demo.empty: No data is currently available for the mini demo.

hero.title: Analysis
hero.subtitle: Analytical reports on PnL, top trades/days, and strategy behavior. This is the quickest way to see where the strategy earns, where it loses, and under which market regimes.

ratings.title: Policy ratings
ratings.description: Best and worst trades/days by outcome, plus summary Equity/DD for top-3 policies. Ideal entry point to find extreme drawdowns and unusually successful periods.

policyBranchMega.description: The most complete metric summary for each policy and branch (BASE/ANTI-D): return, risk, stability, recovery, long/short split, and average growth pace. This is the main table for policy comparison.

dayStats.title: Day statistics
dayStats.description: Breakdowns by day types (UP/DOWN/FLAT) and weekdays: WinRate, NoTrade share, and missed opportunity. Helps identify the regime where the strategy has the strongest edge.

confidenceRisk.title: Confidence and TP/SL
confidenceRisk.description: Bucket statistics by model confidence: TP/SL hit frequency and MFE/MAE dynamics across signal levels. Helps decide where to increase take-profit and where to reduce risk.

executionPipeline.description: End-to-end report by computation layers: model, decision, execution, accounting, and final aggregation. Useful for step-by-step formula validation and mismatch analysis.

page.errorTitle: Failed to load execution pipeline report
page.title: Execution Pipeline
page.subtitle: End-to-end report by layers: model, decision, execution, accounting, and final metric aggregation.

status.title: DEBUG: freshness not verified
status.description: Status endpoint for backtest_execution_pipeline is not configured: report metadata is shown without freshness verification.

sectionDescriptions.modelLevel: Model signal distribution across full history: LONG/SHORT/NO-DIRECTION and their shares. This is the top layer before policy decisions.
sectionDescriptions.decisionLevel: Policy decision layer: how many days were actually traded, coverage, average leverage, and average stake size.
sectionDescriptions.executionLevel: Trade execution layer: entry sources, actual exit reasons, and liquidation reach/trigger facts.
sectionDescriptions.accountingLevel: Accounting reconciliation: positional PnL, fees, net result, and wealth-delta consistency invariants.
sectionDescriptions.aggregationLevel: Final policy-level metric aggregation: TotalPnl, Drawdown, WinRate, liquidations, and withdrawn profit.

invalidGeneratedAt.title: Execution pipeline report has invalid generatedAtUtc
invalidGeneratedAt.description: generatedAtUtc is missing or invalid. Check report serialization.

invalidSource.title: Execution pipeline report source is invalid
invalidSource.description: API source endpoint is missing or invalid. Check VITE_API_BASE_URL / VITE_DEV_API_PROXY_TARGET.

invalidSections.title: Execution pipeline report sections are invalid
invalidSections.description: Report sections were not recognized or are malformed. Check report.sections structure.

hero.title: Diagnostics
hero.subtitle: Map of diagnostic reports: risk, guardrail, decisions, and hotspots. Each block is a dedicated page with detailed tables showing drawdown causes and model errors.

risk.title: Risk and liquidations
risk.description: Equity/DD, liquidations, distance-to-liquidation, and risk metrics. Core base for finding loss causes and assessing stability.

guardrail.description: Filter confusion matrix, rolling thresholds, and economic effect of blocking bad days. Shows whether guardrail protects profit or hurts it.

decisions.title: Decisions / Attribution
decisions.description: Who made the final decision and why: decomposition by actors, reasons, and blame split. Helps determine whether signal quality or policy filters are at fault.

hotspots.description: Hotspots with low coverage, frequent NoTrade, and opposite decisions. Shows where the strategy breaks down and loses days.

other.title: Other
other.description: Rare or new diagnostic tables that are not yet assigned to the main groups.

recent.label: Recent tail
recent.hint: Latest 240 trading days in the active model row

meta.trainingModelFallback: no data (check training section in the report)

clientError.title: Report rendering error
clientError.description: A client-side error occurred while rendering current prediction. The rest of the app keeps working.

globalParams.title: Global parameters
globalParams.description: Base risk settings applied to all policies in baseline run. These values define daily stop-loss and take-profit boundaries.

dailySl.tooltip: Daily loss limit in percent. If price moves further, the position is closed.

dailyTp.tooltip: Daily profit limit in percent. Profit is fixed when this level is reached.

table.title: Policies (baseline, WITH SL)
table.description: Summary table of baseline-run results for each policy. A quick way to compare return and risk before detailed diagnostics.

policy.label: Policy
policy.tooltip: Trading policy name for which final metrics are shown.

margin.label: Margin
margin.tooltip: Trade margin mode: Cross or Isolated.

mode.label: Mode
mode.tooltip: Logic branch: Base or Anti-direction (direction inversion on risk trigger).

totalPnlPct.label: Total PnL, %
totalPnlPct.tooltip: Total policy return in percent over the backtest period.

maxDdPct.label: Max DD, %
maxDdPct.tooltip: Maximum capital drawdown in percent - key risk metric.

liquidations.label: Liquidations
liquidations.tooltip: Whether liquidations occurred (Yes/No). Critical risk flag.

withdrawUsd.label: Withdraw, $
withdrawUsd.tooltip: How much profit was withdrawn from capital (if applicable).

trades.label: Trades
trades.tooltip: Number of trades for the policy over the period.

invalidSource.title: Model stats report source is invalid
invalidSource.message: API source endpoint is missing or invalid. Check VITE_API_BASE_URL / VITE_DEV_API_PROXY_TARGET.

bucketQuery.title: Model stats bucket query is invalid
bucketQuery.message: Query parameter "bucket" is invalid. Expected daily, intraday, delayed, or total.

metricQuery.title: Model stats metric query is invalid
metricQuery.message: Query parameter "metric" is invalid. Expected real or no-biggest-liq-loss.

tpSlQuery.title: Model stats TP/SL query is invalid
tpSlQuery.message: Query parameter "tpsl" is invalid. Expected all, dynamic, or static.

sections.title: Model stats sections are missing
sections.message: Report sections for the selected bucket/metric were not found or are tagged inconsistently.

terms.title: Model stats terms are invalid
terms.message: Failed to build terms for model-stats tables. Check columns and term dictionary.

header.subtitle: Consolidated report on ML-model quality and behavior across train / OOS / full / recent samples. Select segment and report mode first, then inspect detailed metric cards.

oos.label: OOS (fair evaluation)
oos.description: {{label}}: {{count}} records. Measures quality on data not seen during training.

train.label: Train
train.description: {{label}}: {{count}} records. Shows model behavior on the training sample.

full.label: Full history
full.description: {{label}}: {{count}} records. Covers the full available data history.

recent.label: Recent ({{days}} d)
recent.description: {{label}}: {{count}} records. Focuses on the latest tail window.

status.title: DEBUG: freshness not verified
status.message: Status endpoint for backtest_model_stats is not configured: report metadata is shown without freshness verification.

control.title: How to read this report
control.text: Segments (OOS / Train / Full / Recent) define which data is used for metrics. "Business" mode shows aggregated indicators, while "Tech" mode shows detailed confusion matrices and distributions for model analysis.

terms.title: Model stats terms
terms.subtitle: Detailed definitions of all columns used in the current table set.

header.title: Prediction aggregation
header.subtitle: Summary of probabilities and metrics across base and overlay layers (Day / Day+Micro / Total). Segments represent Train / OOS / Recent / Full, followed by detailed stats and recent-day debug.

probs.title: Aggregated probabilities
probs.hint: Average class probabilities by segment. This checks calibration and drift, not prediction hit-rate.

metrics.title: Quality metrics
metrics.hint: Accuracy/Micro‑F1/LogLoss by layer. Confusion matrices show which classes are confused.
metrics.layerHint: Core layer-quality metrics: accuracy, log-loss, and data volume. Hover metrics for definitions.
metrics.confusionHint: Confusion matrix shows how often the layer mixes classes. Rows are true labels, columns are predicted labels.

debug.title: Recent-days debug
debug.hint: Last N days with ground truth and predictions. Flags show where micro/SL actually changed final class or probabilities.
debug.empty: No debug rows. Check aggregation generation consistency.
debug.tableHint: This table helps quickly spot days where micro/SL changed result. Columns include true label, layer predictions, and overlay-impact flags.

segment.hint: The table below shows average class probabilities for this data segment. Compare Day / Day+Micro / Total to see overlay contribution.

probTable.layer: Aggregation stage: Day (base model), Day+Micro (with micro overlay), and Total (with SL overlay).
probTable.pUp: Average probability of Up class for selected layer and segment.
probTable.pFlat: Average probability of Flat class for selected layer and segment.
probTable.pDown: Average probability of Down class for selected layer and segment.
probTable.sum: Average sum P_up+P_flat+P_down. Normally close to 1; strong drift indicates normalization issues.

pills.confDay: Average confidence signal of the base Day layer. This is a separate signal (not probability sum), useful for sanity checks.
pills.confMicro: Average confidence signal of micro layer used inside flat regime. Separate from class probabilities.
pills.slScoreDays: How many days in segment had non-zero SL-score. Shows how often SL model affected the flow.

metrics.accuracy: Share of correct predictions: Correct / N. Higher is better.
metrics.microF1: Micro-F1 for 3-class task. In current implementation it equals Accuracy, but kept as a separate metric.
metrics.logLoss: Average log-loss on probabilities of true class. Lower is better. Only valid records are used.
metrics.nCorrect: N is total records in segment, Correct is number of predictions matching true label.
metrics.validInvalidLogLoss: Records used for log-loss. Invalid > 0 means invalid probabilities (ideally 0).

confusion.truePred: Confusion matrix: rows are true labels, columns are predicted labels. Values are day counts.

labels.flat: FLAT class: sideways day without strong trend.

debug.dateUtc: Trading day date (UTC) for this row.
debug.true: True day class (fact): UP / FLAT / DOWN.
debug.day: Prediction of base day model (before micro/SL overlays).
debug.dayMicro: Prediction after micro overlay is applied.
debug.pDay: Probability triple Up/Flat/Down for base Day model.
debug.pDayMicro: Probability triple Up/Flat/Down after micro overlay.
debug.pTotal: Final Up/Flat/Down probabilities after SL overlay.
debug.microUsed: Flag that micro overlay actually changed prediction (Day → Day+Micro).
debug.slUsed: Flag that SL overlay affected final outcome (Day+Micro → Total).
debug.microAgree: Whether Day and Day+Micro labels match (true = unchanged).
debug.slPenLong: SL overlay reduced long probability (penalty to P_up).
debug.slPenShort: SL overlay reduced short probability (penalty to P_down).

page.errorTitle: Failed to load baseline summary and backtest profiles

header.title: Backtest Full: profiles, what-if, and A/B comparison
header.subtitle: What-if, baseline (in dynamic/static), and A/B run one-shot previews with the current backend config, including dynamic TP/SL, confidence gating, and policy ratios.
header.tpSlSliceHint: `ALL` runs merged mode. `DYNAMIC` and `STATIC` run separate simulations: dynamic TP/SL only or static TP/SL only.

errors.baselineProfileMissing: Baseline profile is missing or does not contain config for selected TP/SL mode.
errors.baselineModePreviewFailed: Failed to run baseline preview/full for selected TP/SL mode.
errors.compareProfilesRequired: Both profiles must be selected for A/B comparison.
errors.baselineMetricsBlockDescription: Failed to render baseline/preview comparison metrics.

baselineColumn.loadingSummary: Recalculating baseline summary for selected TP/SL mode...
baselineColumn.emptySummary: No baseline summary for selected TP/SL mode.

errors.summaryDescription: Summary for selected TP/SL mode is unavailable.

whatIfColumn.title: What-if profile configuration
whatIfColumn.previewPolicyRatiosSubtitle: Metrics are calculated from the same what-if run shown in summary.

errors.clientDescription: What-if block failed to render on the client. Baseline and profile comparison remain available.

baselineSl.tooltip: Base daily stop-loss. If dynamic TP/SL is not activated for the day, this value is used.

baselineTp.tooltip: Base daily take-profit. Dynamic overlay scales it only when confidence rules allow it.

dynamic.infoEnabled: Dynamic TP/SL is applied only when confidence bucket passes historical constraints (minimum samples + minimum win-rate). Baseline TP/SL is used on all other days.

tpMultMin.tooltip: Lower multiplier of dynamic TP relative to baseline TP (fraction, not percent).

tpMultMax.tooltip: Upper multiplier of dynamic TP relative to baseline TP.

slMultMin.tooltip: Lower multiplier of dynamic SL relative to baseline SL.

slMultMax.tooltip: Upper multiplier of dynamic SL relative to baseline SL.

dailyTpMin.tooltip: Hard lower clamp for final dynamic TP after all multipliers.

dailyTpMax.tooltip: Hard upper clamp for final dynamic TP.

dailySlMin.tooltip: Hard lower clamp for final dynamic SL.

dailySlMax.tooltip: Hard upper clamp for final dynamic SL.

minBucketSamples.tooltip: Minimum historical observations in confidence bucket required to allow dynamic TP/SL.

minBucketWinRate.tooltip: Minimum historical win-rate in confidence bucket required to enable dynamic TP/SL.

confGateMin.tooltip: Minimum directional confidence required to allow a trade.

confGateRiskDayMin.tooltip: Minimum confidence for RiskDay. If 0, Conf gate min is used.

dynamic.infoDisabled: This profile has confidence-risk overlay disabled or missing. Preview runs with baseline TP/SL without dynamic modifiers.

policyTable.title: Leverage policies
policyTable.subtitle: Select policies included in preview and adjust const leverage if needed.

enabled.label: Enabled
enabled.tooltip: Enable or disable policy in the current one-shot preview.

type.label: Type
type.tooltip: Policy logic type (const, risk_aware, ultra_safe, dynamic, spot).

leverage.label: Leverage
leverage.tooltip: For const policies leverage can be edited manually. Other policy types ignore this field.

margin.label: Margin
margin.tooltip: Policy margin mode: Cross or Isolated.

policyRatios.title: Policy metrics (Sharpe / Sortino / PnL)
policyRatios.subtitle: Policy comparison inside selected capital bucket. Full coverage requires checking daily/intraday/delayed.

bucket.label: Bucket
bucket.tooltip: Capital bucket: daily, intraday, delayed, or total.

trades.label: Trades
trades.tooltip: Number of trades in selected bucket.

pnl.label: PnL %
pnl.tooltip: Total policy return in selected bucket, %.

errors.clientTitle: Profile comparison block is temporarily unavailable
errors.clientDescription: A client-side error occurred while rendering A/B comparison. Baseline and What-if remain available.

compareSection.previewEmpty: No preview result yet for profile {{profile}}.
compareSection.policyRatiosSubtitle: Metrics are built from the same one-shot run as profile {{profile}} summary.

chart.title: Mega table chart explorer
chart.subtitle: Charts do not replace the source-value table. This mode provides a first reading layer: ranking, risk/return scatter, and drill-down into the selected Policy.

ranking.title: Policy-configuration ranking by selected metric
ranking.subtitle: Selecting a ranking bar opens the selected Policy for detailed reading and comparison of its Branch / SL Mode configurations.
ranking.emptyTitle: No Policy configurations for selected metric
ranking.emptyDescription: After branch/SL/search filters there are no Policy configurations where the selected metric has a numeric value.

scatter.title: Risk / return scatter
scatter.subtitle: X and Y axes are selected independently, so return, drawdown, recovery, and growth-rate metrics can be compared in one field.
scatter.emptyDescription: No Policy configurations remain where both selected metrics are available at the same time.

detail.subtitle: A Policy was selected from the chart. Below are the values of the selected Branch / SL Mode configuration and the comparison of the same Policy across other execution variants.
detail.policyCompareSubtitle: This bar chart uses the same ranking metric and shows how the selected Policy changes across Branch / SL Mode configurations.
detail.policyCompareEmptyTitle: Selected Policy has no values for the ranking metric
detail.policyCompareEmptyDescription: Other Branch / SL Mode configurations exist for this Policy, but they do not contain the currently selected ranking metric.
detail.empty: After selecting a ranking bar or scatter point, this panel opens the selected Policy and the values of its current Branch / SL Mode configuration.

items.item1: Backtest is a simulation of how the strategy would trade on historical data.
items.item2: All Policy configurations are computed on the same period and identical source data, so Policy-to-Policy comparison is valid.
items.item3: Differences between Policy configurations reflect strategy-rule differences, not differences in market history.

items.policy: Policy: when to open a position, in which direction to open (LONG, SHORT, or skip day), what size to use, and where TP/SL are set.
items.branch:
Branch is an execution scenario for the same Policy.

BASE: uses original daily signal direction without inversion (LONG stays LONG, SHORT stays SHORT).

ANTI-D: anti-direction rule is checked daily. If triggered, direction is forcibly inverted (LONG -> SHORT, SHORT -> LONG).

One mandatory anti-direction filter: MinMove in working range (0.5%-12%).
items.slMode: SL Mode: WITH-SL enables stop-loss (exit priority liquidation -> stop-loss -> take-profit -> EndOfDay). NO-SL disables stop-loss (exit only by take-profit, liquidation, or EndOfDay); this can wipe bucket capital and, in cross margin, the entire trading balance.
items.tpSlMode: TP/SL mode: ALL shows all trades, DYNAMIC shows only trades with actually applied dynamic levels, STATIC shows only trades with fixed levels.
items.zonal: ZONAL: WITH applies zonal entry filter, WITHOUT runs without this filter.
items.metricView: METRIC VIEW: REAL shows factual result, NO BIGGEST LIQ LOSS removes one largest liquidation only for tail-risk analysis. This is counterfactual mode, not real final return.
items.bucket: bucket: daily/intraday/delayed are independent slices with separate capital; total is aggregate sum of their metrics.

reading.title: How to read / interpret the table

items.item1: Reading order: 1) emergency risk first (HadLiq and AccRuin), 2) then drawdown depth (MaxDD%), 3) then recovery difficulty (Recovered, RecovDays, ReqGain%), 4) only after that final return (TotalPnl% and Wealth%).
items.item2: Comparison is valid only inside one slice: same bucket, TP/SL mode, SL Mode, ZONAL, and Metric View.
items.item3: If NO BIGGEST LIQ LOSS is significantly better than REAL, strategy result critically depends on one extreme failure.

items.item1: 3.1 Position (trade): LONG is a bet on price growth. SHORT is a bet on price decline. If filters block the day, no position is opened (no-trade).
items.item2: 3.2 Leverage: multiplier over margin. 10x leverage means ~1% price move gives ~10% margin-level result.
items.item3: 3.3 Margin: funds allocated to position. Isolated limits risk to that position margin. Cross uses shared deposit as margin, and liquidation can wipe full balance.
items.item4: 3.4 Stop-Loss and Take-Profit: Stop-Loss auto-closes on loss, Take-Profit auto-locks profit.
items.item5: 3.5 Liquidation: exchange force-closes position when loss almost consumes margin. In Isolated, position margin is lost; in Cross, liquidation may wipe full deposit.
items.item6: 3.6 Backtest: strategy check on history. Useful for idea/risk comparison, but does not guarantee future behavior.

simulation.title: What simulation includes

items.item1: Exchange fees: included (round-trip, entry and exit fees).
items.item2: Funding: not included, so real exchange result may differ for long-held positions.
items.item4Prefix: Event order inside first-event rule: liquidation → stop-loss → take-profit →

simulation.endOfDayTooltip: EndOfDay (EOD) means time-based close at end of trading window if TP, SL, or liquidation did not trigger earlier.

table.part1Description: Part 1/4: base summary by days, risk days, leverage/cap fraction, and core PnL metrics (up to MaxDD_Ratio%).
table.part2Description: Part 2/4: balance and recovery metrics (Withdrawn/OnExch, liquidations, recovery, DD70).
table.part3Description: Part 3/4: horizon and average growth rates, long/short split, and diagnostic counters.
table.part4Description: Part 4/4: trade-derived and advanced daily-series metrics (ProfitFactor, Expectancy, Best/Worst day, PSR, MinTRL).
table.mergedAllDescription: Summary metrics of merged SL slice.
table.mergedSelectedDescription: Summary metrics of selected SL slice.
